title: How to Get Rid of Swollen Feet with 7 Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins are good for swollen feet because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain by narrowing blood vessels and preventing fluid from building up in the feet.
Polyphenols are good for swollen feet because they help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can ease swelling and discomfort in the feet.
Hamamelitannins are good for swollen feet because they help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected area, which can alleviate swelling and discomfort.
Quercetin is good for swollen feet because it helps reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which in turn can ease the swelling and discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for swollen feet because they help to improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and relax blood vessels, which in turn eases the swelling and discomfort in the feet.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana cream is a natural remedy that can help with swollen feet. This is because it reduces inflammation and soothes the skin. When you apply the cream, the active ingredients in Hamamelis virginiana work to calm the affected area. The cream helps to reduce swelling by improving blood flow and reducing the amount of fluid that builds up in the feet. As a result, it relieves pain and discomfort caused by swollen feet. It also helps to prevent further swelling by reducing the amount of fluid that leaks out of the blood vessels. By reducing inflammation, Hamamelis virginiana cream also helps to prevent the formation of fluid-filled blisters that can be painful and uncomfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for swollen feet, you'll need to start by infusing dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. This process is called cold infusion, and it helps to gently extract the beneficial properties from the herbs. Simply place a handful of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in a clean glass jar, pour in the carrier oil, and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Next, you'll need to mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a creamy texture. Melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler, then slowly add in the infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture has cooled and thickened. Finally, transfer the cream to a clean jar and store it in a cool, dark place, where it will remain fresh for several months. To use the cream, simply apply a generous amount to your swollen feet, massaging it in thoroughly to help reduce inflammation and promote healthy circulation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is good for swollen feet because it helps to reduce swelling and ease pain. The cooling properties of aloe vera help to relax tense muscles and reduce inflammation. When you apply aloe barbadensis cream to your swollen feet, the gel-like substance absorbs quickly into the skin and starts to work its magic. It helps to increase blood circulation, which gets rid of excess fluid that causes swelling. Additionally, aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and pain. It also has moisturizing properties that soften and soothe dry skin, making it more comfortable to walk on. Using aloe barbadensis cream regularly can also help to prevent future swelling by keeping your skin healthy and hydrated.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for swollen feet, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need to dry and grind 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder. You can do this by spreading the leaves on a baking sheet and drying them in a low-temperature oven at 150°F (65°C) for 2-3 hours, or by using a food dehydrator. Next, mix the Ginkgo powder with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture until the beeswax has melted, then add in 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Continue stirring until the mixture has cooled and thickened, then transfer it to a small container. Let the cream cool and harden completely before use. To use the cream, simply apply a generous amount to the affected area and massage it in until it's fully absorbed. The Ginkgo biloba in the cream may help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet, making it a great natural remedy for swollen feet.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana cream is a natural remedy that can help with swollen feet. It contains special ingredients that reduce swelling and pain. The cream works by stopping the body's natural response to injury, which is to send extra blood to the affected area. This extra blood causes swelling and pain. Arnica montana cream helps to break down the blood clots that cause swelling, and it reduces inflammation. When applied to swollen feet, the cream can help to ease discomfort and make it easier to move around. It's also good for relieving stress and strain on the feet, which can be caused by activities like running or standing for long periods.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for swollen feet, you'll need to gather some ingredients and follow a few simple steps. First, infuse dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, by combining one part of the leaves with two parts of the oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture gently over low heat for about 30 minutes, then let it cool. Next, strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids. In a separate bowl, mix together a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter with a moisturizing ingredient like aloe vera gel or coconut oil. Then, slowly pour the infused oil into the thickening mixture while whipping it with a hand mixer until it thickens into a creamy texture. Finally, transfer the cream into a jar and store it in the fridge to prevent spoilage. To use, simply apply a small amount to your swollen feet and massage it in thoroughly, letting the soothing properties of Ginkgo biloba work their magic to reduce swelling and ease discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is good for swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties. It helps reduce redness and swelling by soothing the skin. This is because Calendula contains compounds that calm the skin and reduce irritation. When applied to swollen feet, Calendula cream can ease discomfort and pain. It also promotes healing by protecting the skin from further damage. Additionally, Calendula cream has antiseptic properties, which help prevent infections that can make swelling worse. By reducing swelling and promoting healing, Calendula cream can help you feel more comfortable and mobile. Calendula cream is also gentle on the skin, making it suitable for people with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for swollen feet, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a few drops of tea tree oil for its antifungal properties. First, mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Heat the mixture until the beeswax melts, then remove it from the heat and let it cool slightly. Next, add 2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to a small bowl and pour 1 tablespoon of hot water over them. Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid and discard the solids. Add the Ginkgo biloba liquid to the coconut oil and beeswax mixture, along with a few drops of vitamin E oil and tea tree oil. Stir the mixture well and pour it into a small container. Let the cream cool and solidify before using it on your swollen feet. Apply a thin layer of the cream to your feet and massage it in to help reduce swelling and soothe tired feet.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a popular remedy for swollen feet. This is because it contains licorice extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied to swollen feet, it helps to reduce swelling and ease pain. The cream also has moisturizing properties, which keeps the skin hydrated and soft. This is especially important for people who spend a lot of time on their feet, such as nurses, chefs, or anyone who does a lot of walking or standing. The anti-inflammatory properties in Glycyrrhiza glabra cream also help to reduce redness and itchiness that can be associated with swollen feet. Additionally, it has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing. When used regularly, Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can help to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet, making it a great solution for people who experience this common problem.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal cream that helps with swollen feet, start by combining 1 cup of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of water in a large pot. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Next, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. Allow the liquid to cool, then mix it with 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler. Heat the mixture until the beeswax is melted, then remove it from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel to the mixture, stirring well to combine. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before applying it to your feet to help reduce swelling and soothe dry skin.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams that help with swollen feet, start by gathering a few simple ingredients. You'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and a small saucepan. Begin by infusing the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container every day to help the herbs release their goodness. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil and discard the leaves. In a small saucepan, melt the beeswax and shea butter over low heat, then add the infused oil and vitamin E oil. Stir the mixture well and continue heating until the beeswax has fully melted. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken. Once it's reached a smooth, creamy consistency, pour it into a container and let it cool completely before using it to massage your swollen feet. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ginkgo biloba will help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, while the carrier oil and beeswax will moisturize and protect your skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams can be helpful for swollen feet due to its ability to improve blood circulation. When blood flows properly, it helps reduce swelling. Ginkgo biloba contains antioxidants that can help repair damaged blood vessels, allowing for better circulation. This can be especially helpful for people who stand or walk a lot, as it can reduce the pressure on their feet. The cream can also help to reduce inflammation, which is another cause of swelling. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have anti-edema properties, which means it can help to reduce the amount of fluid that builds up in the feet, leading to swelling. By applying Ginkgo biloba cream to the affected area, it can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams that help with swollen feet, you will need some basic ingredients. First, dry and grind 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle. Then, mix the Ginkgo powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir until the beeswax has melted, then remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Next, add 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil to the mixture, stirring until well combined. Finally, transfer the mixture to a glass jar and let it cool and thicken completely before using it on your swollen feet. To use the cream, simply apply a generous amount to your feet and massage it in, paying special attention to any areas that are particularly swollen or sore. Leave the cream on overnight and wash it off in the morning with warm water for best results.

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be very helpful in reducing swelling in the feet. One of the herbs used in these creams is Hamamelis virginiana, also known as witch hazel. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and soothe aching feet. When applied topically, it can help to improve blood circulation and reduce the appearance of varicose veins, which can contribute to swelling.  Another herb used in foot creams is Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera. Aloe vera is known for its moisturizing and soothing properties, which can help to soften and cool the skin. It can also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, which can be beneficial for people with foot injuries or conditions such as plantar fasciitis.  Swollen feet can be a real issue, and it's not just a matter of aesthetics. When feet are swollen, it can be painful to walk or stand for long periods of time, which can limit your ability to engage in activities you enjoy. Swollen feet can also be a sign of underlying health issues, such as poor circulation or diabetes. By addressing the issue of swollen feet, you can improve your overall health and well-being, and enjoy a better quality of life.  Fixing the issue of swollen feet can have a significant impact on your daily life. You can walk longer distances without feeling tired or in pain, which can allow you to pursue hobbies and interests that you enjoy. You can also engage in physical activities, such as sports or exercise, without worrying about discomfort or pain in your feet. By using herbal creams and addressing the underlying causes of swelling, you can take control of your foot health and improve your overall quality of life.
supplementary_best: A combination of Aloe barbadensis, Arnica montana, and Hamamelis virginiana creams can be effective in remedying swollen feet. Aloe barbadensis provides soothing and moisturizing properties that help to reduce inflammation and ease pain. Arnica montana has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce swelling and promote healing. Hamamelis virginiana, also known as witch hazel, helps to reduce swelling and ease pain due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The combination of these herbs in cream form can provide a cooling and soothing effect on the skin, while also reducing inflammation and promoting healing. When applied topically to the swollen area, the cream can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing, making it a useful remedy for swollen feet.
supplementary_avoid: When using herbal creams for swollen feet, some ingredients can actually make the problem worse. One of these is Devil's Claw (Harpagophytum procumbens). It's known to increase blood pressure and heart rate, which can worsen swelling and make circulation issues worse. Another herb to avoid is Willow Bark (Salix alba), which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. While it might provide temporary pain relief, it can also cause stomach problems and interact with medications, making it a bad choice for people with swollen feet.  Capsicum (Capsicum annuum) is another ingredient to be cautious with. It can cause skin irritation and increase heart rate, which can exacerbate swelling. Feverfew (Tanacetum parthenium) also contains compounds that can cause skin reactions and interact with blood-thinning medications, making it a bad choice for people with swollen feet. Finally, Witch Hazel (Hamamelis virginiana - see the list of avoided herbs)  is not mentioned, but  we can mention another similar herb:  Meadowsweet (Filipendula ulmaria) - it contains salicylic acid, which, like willow bark, can cause stomach problems and interact with medications.  These herbal creams can cause more harm than good, especially if you have pre-existing medical conditions or take medications. It's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new products.
supplementary_other_remedies: Epsom salt baths can help reduce swelling in the feet. The magnesium in Epsom salt helps relax the body and improve blood circulation, which reduces swelling. To use Epsom salt baths, add about two pounds of Epsom salt to warm water and soak your feet for 15-20 minutes.  Cold compresses or ice packs can also help reduce swelling. When you apply cold compresses to your feet, the cold temperature causes the blood vessels to constrict, which reduces blood flow to the area and helps reduce swelling. You can make a cold compress by wetting a cloth with cold water, wrapping it in a plastic bag, and applying it to your feet.  Foot elevators or wedges can help reduce swelling by allowing your feet to rest in an elevated position. When you elevate your feet, the swelling in your feet decreases because gravity helps reduce fluid buildup. You can use a foam wedge or a book to elevate your feet while sitting or sleeping.  Elevating your feet above the level of your heart can also help reduce swelling. When you elevate your feet, the fluid in your feet is drawn back into your body, which reduces swelling. You can use a pillow or a stack of books to elevate your feet while sitting or sleeping.  Wearing compression socks or stockings can help reduce swelling by applying gentle pressure to your feet. Compression socks or stockings are designed to compress the feet, which helps reduce blood flow to the area and reduces swelling.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with swollen feet may also experience pain in their legs, ankles, or feet due to poor circulation. This can be caused by a condition called peripheral artery disease, where the arteries in the legs become narrowed or blocked. Medicinal herbs like ginkgo biloba and turmeric can help improve circulation by reducing inflammation and increasing blood flow. Ginkgo biloba has compounds that help relax blood vessels, allowing more blood to flow to the legs and feet.  Some people with swollen feet may also experience varicose veins, where the veins in the legs become swollen and twisted. This can be caused by a combination of poor circulation, pregnancy, and genetics. The medicinal herb horse chestnut can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the legs, which can help alleviate varicose veins. It contains a compound called saponin, which helps to strengthen the walls of the veins and improve circulation.  In addition, people with swollen feet may experience diabetes or high blood pressure, which can lead to nerve damage in the feet. The medicinal herb prickly pear cactus can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can help alleviate nerve damage. It contains a compound called opuntia, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the nerves.  Finally, people with swollen feet may experience fungal infections like athlete's foot. The medicinal herb tea tree oil can help reduce fungal growth and alleviate symptoms. It contains a compound called terpinen-4-ol, which has antifungal properties that can help kill fungal spores and reduce inflammation.
